anti_claim: This standard governs public executable-doctrine grammar fixtures and imported public grammar specimens only; it does not publish private doctrine bodies, prove doctrine completeness, authorize provider calls, or grant release/publication authority.

allowed_public_material: expected negative-case ids and error codes
forbidden_public_material: raw operator notes or raw seed bodies
provider request or response payload bodies

receipt_expectations: report accepted and rejected standard rows
report imported executable-grammar metabolism source refs, artifact refs, and digests without duplicating body text
report source_module_imports and source_open_body_imports for copied executable-grammar macro specimen bodies
include authority ceiling, anti-claim, and secret-exclusion status

standard_payload.anti_purpose: A prose-only doctrine note, a receipt archive, or a validator that passes while copied macro specimen bodies remain unconsumed.
failure_modes: private doctrine body exported as public fixture
standard row accepted without teleology, governing standard, receipt expectations, or anti-claim
paper module accepted without governing standard or receipt expectations
macro executable-grammar specimen imported without exact source-to-target digest checks
provider replay bridge treated as real provider transcript or reliability proof
receipt duplicates macro body text instead of carrying refs, hashes, and verdicts
standard_payload.ontology: A public-safe grammar runtime that validates executable standard metadata, paper-module shape, negative-case boundaries, and copied macro executable-grammar specimen bodies.
standard_payload.teleology: Keep doctrine grammar executable and source-open without letting grammar validation become doctrine completeness, private-root equivalence, or release authority.

validation_rules: require standard rows to carry teleology, governing standard, receipt expectations, and anti-claim
reject prose-only standards that claim executable authority
reject grammar or paper-module fixtures that claim doctrine completeness
reject copied macro doctrine or provider payload bodies in public fixtures
require imported executable-grammar metabolism specimens to pass exact digest and source-ref checks
require imported executable-grammar metabolism specimens to expose source_open_body_imports body-floor accounting from source_module_manifest.json
require receipts to omit body text and expose only refs, hashes, counts, authority ceilings, and verdicts
